week_01: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Nine category wins and a 9-1 demolition of the Roughnecks — Schanuel, Abreu, Happ, and Baldwin all went multi-HR in the same week, making this the deepest and most balanced offensive roster in the league | same

2. BaseballPro77 | An 8-2 blowout win powered by Murakami's 3 HR, Trout's 2 HR, and Stanton contributing — the rotation remains inconsistent but the sheer offensive ceiling here is unmatched when everyone produces together | up

3. General Hospital | A 4-4 tie against a Wonderdogs team they should have beaten stings, but a .414 OBP, 6 HR, 23 RBI, and 3 QS all in one week confirms this is a complete roster firing on nearly every cylinder | same

4. Albuquerque Bombers | A 6-4 win built on pitching efficiency — Alcantara and Rogers each delivered quality starts with WHIPs under 1.00, and Yelich's .600 OBP anchored a lineup that won without hitting a single home run | up

5. North Correa | A convincing 7-3 win with 23 runs scored and four stolen bases; Arozarena and Hoerner are difference-makers in the speed categories and the rotation posted a 2.73 ERA with two quality starts | same

6. Athletic Park Dragons | A 5-4 win that required every ounce of their pitching staff's performance — Sánchez's 0.50 WHIP and Joe Ryan's 0.56 WHIP are elite, but the offense's .301 OBP is dangerously thin for sustained success | same

7. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A 4-4 tie against General Hospital is genuinely impressive given the firepower they faced, and Donovan's 2 HR plus three quality starts show real roster depth, but ties don't move standings | up

8. Hiroshima Radiation | Lost 5-4 in a tight battle and still posted a 2.35 ERA and 44 strikeouts as a staff — this team keeps pitching well enough to win and keeps finding ways not to; the offense's 0 SB and .304 OBP are the culprits | down

9. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 6-4 despite 6 HR and 5 SB — the pitching staff imploded with a 6.00 ERA and zero quality starts, and Detmers and Peralta both got roughed up; this roster's floor is worryingly low when the arms fail | down

10. Riverside Roughnecks | Got obliterated 9-1 by the Skeeters and the pitching staff's 7.91 ERA tells the whole story; Langeliers' 3 HR and Royce Lewis's 2 HR are encouraging offensive signs but the rotation is in crisis | down

11. Canberra Wombats | Lost 8-2 and posted zero HR in a power-heavy league — Dylan Cease's 0.94 WHIP was wasted, Logan Webb's 10.80 ERA was disqualifying, and a roster without home run hitters cannot compete in this field | same

12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 7-3 and the rotation posted a 5.79 ERA with Matthew Boyd's 14.73 ERA being genuinely alarming; dropping McGreevy without a meaningful replacement shows a front office still searching for answers | same

13. Buttville Skidmarks | Gausman's 0.17 WHIP and DeLauter's 4 HR were spectacular individual performances that still produced a 7-3 loss — category imbalance is this team's defining flaw and it cost them a winnable matchup | down

14. Benchwarmers | Won 7-3 on fumes — zero quality starts, a 4.50 ERA, and a 23-hit week is not a formula for sustained success; Zach Neto is a genuine star but one player cannot carry this roster into the playoffs | same
week_02: 1. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Historic 9-0 shutout anchored by Ben Rice's monster week and Mason Miller's 0.30 WHIP; this team just announced it belongs at the top | up

2. Sugar Land Skeeters | Most consistent team in the league across all four weeks, 8-1 category win this week, Drake Baldwin and Matt Olson both mashing with Chase Burns dealing — the floor here is elite | same
3. BaseballPro77 | Gutted out a 5-4 win on fumes offensively, but the roster depth and active management keep them near the top; the ERA concern is real and needs fixing fast | down
4. General Hospital | Six category wins with a 2.10 ERA and only 5 HR — this offense has legitimate power gaps but the pitching and on-base skills carry them; Kumar Rocker addition could be huge | up
5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Yordan Alvarez's .600 OBP and a staff-wide 2.25 ERA powered a 6-4 win; tied the league K record and the rotation with Ashcraft, Luzardo, and Burke is genuinely deep | up
6. Riverside Roughnecks | Exploded for 38 runs and 10 HR in a 7-3 win — Otto Lopez, Xavier Edwards, Jackson Merrill, Kerry Carpenter all contributing; is this the breakout week they needed? | up
7. Athletic Park Dragons | Lost 6-4 despite Judge, Wood, and Duran all scoring runs; the .267 OBP is alarming and 46 IP with 46 K is thin rotation usage — needs more from the arms | down
8. North Correa | Oneil Cruz went off for 4 HR and 10 RBI and they still only won 7-3; rotation outside Senga needs reinforcement and the Austin Wells addition at catcher is smart | same
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Kyle Harrison's 26 K and 1.65 ERA was absolutely elite but Freddie Freeman's .226 OBP is deeply concerning; four waiver adds show urgency and energy from the manager | same

10. Hiroshima Radiation | Dropped 8-1 in a bloodbath despite Carlos Correa scoring 8 runs and Schwarber going deep 3 times; a 43 K staff week is the core problem and Colin Rea won't fix it alone | down
11. Canberra Wombats | Lost 6-4 but Altuve's .577 OBP, Walker, Henderson, and Díaz all went deep — the offense is real; Aaron Nola and Seth Lugo both getting torched is the recurring nightmare | same

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Lost 7-3 despite a 3.19 ERA and 4 QS — the pitching did its job and still couldn't cover for the offense; Parker Messick and Gausman both brilliant and it didn't matter | same
13. Benchwarmers | Won 5-4 but this is a smoke-and-mirrors record; Corbin Carroll and Cody Bellinger are real pieces but the rotation logged only 46 IP and the power numbers are near-invisible | same

14. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Got blanked 9-0 in every single category — zero wins, zero stolen bases, a .270 OBP, and Imanaga struggled again; the aggressive waiver moves including Masyn Winn and Dustin May signal panic mode | down
week_03: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | Led the league with 8 category wins for the second time in three weeks, Max Muncy's 3 HR and Michael Wacha's 0.60 ERA anchored a 31-run offensive week and a 2.11 team ERA — this roster is the class of the league | same

2. Hiroshima Radiation | Exploded for an 8-2 demolition behind Taj Bradley's 17 K, Tarik Skubal's 14 K, and a 75-strikeout team total that matched the league's best-ever single-week mark; Bryce Harper's .640 OBP headline an offense that is suddenly clicking at full volume | up

3. Athletic Park Dragons | James Wood's .655 OBP and 3 HR, Willson Contreras' 9 RBI, and Dansby Swanson's 8 runs all in the same week is as dominant an offensive performance as the league has seen; the pitching needs repair but this offense alone keeps them in the top three | same

4. BaseballPro77 | Jordan Walker's 5 HR week and Logan Gilbert's 2.08 ERA anchored a 5-3 win; this team keeps finding production from unexpected sources and has now won in back-to-back weeks with a deep, versatile roster | same

5. General Hospital | Cam Schlittler's 15 K is a tantalizing glimpse at rotation upside and Kumar Rocker is now in the fold, but a 4-4 tie against the Skidmarks when you threw 70 K and posted 5 NSVH is a painful missed opportunity; the pitching is elite, the offense needs consistency | down

6. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Lost 6-3 but their pitching was genuinely excellent — Kris Bubic's 0.43 WHIP, Brandon Woodruff's return, and a 1.65 ERA with 10 NSVH are signs of a staff quietly becoming one of the league's best; offense is the limiting factor | up

7. Albuquerque Bombers | Brice Turang's .474 OBP and 4 steals combined contributions, Sandy Alcantara's 10 K, and four waiver additions show an active front office; lost 5-4 in a close one but the roster is being aggressively upgraded week over week | same

8. North Correa | Shohei Ohtani's 3 HR and Oneil Cruz's 6 SB are as exciting as any individual performances in the league, and 11 stolen bases as a team ties the season record — but the pitching allowed a 4.73 ERA and just 2 QS, which cost them the matchup | down

9. Riverside Roughnecks | Won 6-3 over a Benchwarmers squad without logging a single quality start — zero QS and a 5.04 ERA should not produce wins, but Trevor Story's 9 RBI and Jackson Merrill's 3 steals carried the offense enough to survive; this team is more fragile than their record suggests | same

10. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Lost 8-2 in a rout and the -0.35 luck delta can't explain away a shutout in most pitching categories; Yordan Alvarez and Gunnar Henderson — wait, Henderson is on the Wombats — the offense has talent but the 0 NSVH week is a structural problem | down

11. Buttville Skidmarks | Elly De La Cruz was electric with 4 SB and 2 HR but the team still couldn't break 22 RBI; a 4-4 tie is better than a loss but the rotation is thin after MacKenzie Gore and the offense needs more than one reliable power source | same

12. Canberra Wombats | Gunnar Henderson's 4 HR and Jacob deGrom's 1.64 ERA were genuinely impressive and yet the team lost 8-2 — when your best individual performers can't move the team score, the supporting cast is too weak; dropping Roki Sasaki is a puzzling decision | down

13. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Won 5-4 despite pitching brilliantly all week, which should feel better than it does; the -0.60 luck delta is a flashing warning sign and the offense — 25 runs, 6 HR — remains too quiet to trust against better opponents, though the waiver activity adding Dollander shows intent | up

14. Benchwarmers | Dropped three pitchers including Scherzer in the same week their staff posted a 5.53 ERA and 34 strikeouts — the roster is being dismantled rather than rebuilt, and without those arms the ceiling drops even further; the +0.91 luck delta this week is the only thing keeping them off the basement floor | same
week_04: 1. BaseballPro77 | Record-setting 19 HR and 39 RBI week with a 2.33 ERA confirms this is the most complete roster in the league right now — Trout, Murakami, and Riley are a top-three power trio in the game | up

2. Sugar Land Skeeters | Still the most consistent team over four weeks with category wins totals of 9, 8, 8, and 4 — one off week doesn't erase the best overall resume in the league, and Weathers (18 K) reminded everyone the staff has weapons | same

3. General Hospital | An 8-2 demolition powered by Will Warren (17 K, 1.69 ERA), league-leading 6 QS, and a 2.68 ERA — this rotation is the best in the league and Kumar Rocker hasn't even thrown a pitch yet | up

4. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Paul Skenes (0.90 ERA, 0.50 WHIP), Imanaga, and Framber Valdez combined for a filthy 8 QS and a 2.49 ERA — the pitching staff is genuinely elite and the offense is starting to show up around it | up

5. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge (5 HR), James Wood, and José Caballero anchored another strong offensive week, and the rotation finally delivered a 3.01 ERA with 5 QS — this feels like the team clicking into gear | same

6. Albuquerque Bombers | Won 5-4 with Byron Buxton (3 HR) and a balanced lineup carrying the load — four waiver adds show an aggressive front office, and the 8 category wins in Week 5 history suggests a hot streak may be brewing | same

7. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | José Ramírez (4 HR, 5 SB, .533 OBP) and Yordan Alvarez (4 HR) is one of the most dangerous one-two punches in the league, and Reid Detmers' 1.29 ERA hints at a rotation stabilizing | up

8. Hiroshima Radiation | Tarik Skubal and Nathan Eovaldi both posted sub-1.50 ERAs and the team ERA was a sparkling 2.81, but a -1 NSVH tanked the pitching side and cost them the matchup — the saves situation is a real problem | down

9. North Correa | Lost 5-2 despite Chris Sale (0.86 WHIP) and solid contributions from Nico Hoerner (11 RBI) — the pitching ERA of 5.56 is the culprit, and Austin Wells replacing Realmuto is a meaningful upgrade worth watching | same

10. Riverside Roughnecks | A 4-4 tie when a win was there for the taking is the story — Jacob Misiorowski (14 K) and Raisel Iglesias (3 NSVH) showed real weapons, but the offense left runs on the table all week | same

11. Canberra Wombats | Jo Adell (2 HR, 8 RBI) and Pete Crow-Armstrong were bright spots, but a 4.35 ERA, only 3 QS, and a .283 OBP won't win matchups — adding JR Ritchie while dropping Sasaki is a puzzling rotation decision | same

12. Buttville Skidmarks | Junior Caminero (3 HR) had a strong week but MacKenzie Gore's 6.52 ERA and Gausman's 4.09 ERA tanked the pitching categories — six category wins added to Week 5's history suggests a bounce-back may be coming | up

13. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got historically embarrassed by BaseballPro77's record-setting week, but Bryan Woo (2 QS) and Mason Miller (4 NSVH, 0.50 WHIP) showed this team has real relief-side assets to build around | same

14. Benchwarmers | Michael King (0.82 ERA) was outstanding and Cody Bellinger (.481 OBP) continues to be productive, but 4 HR and only 45 hits in a 10-category format means an 8-2 drubbing was almost inevitable — the roster needs a power injection badly | down
week_05: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | 52 runs and 15 HR remain the most jaw-dropping offensive week of the season; this lineup has no clear weakness and the Skeeters have been top-two in category wins every single week | same

2. General Hospital | A frustrating 5-5 tie despite Wrobleski (0.69 ERA) and Fried (0.63 WHIP) carrying the rotation; Kumar Rocker now added to an already elite staff makes this team the most frightening on paper | same

3. Albuquerque Bombers | The 8-1 demolition of the Reapers was the statement win of the week — Harrison and Roupp were unhittable, Jung and Lile added immediate lineup value, and four waiver adds show a manager locked in and building | up

4. Hiroshima Radiation | McLean's 17 K and 12 team NSVH are legitimate category weapons; a 5-5 tie that felt winnable stings, but the saves-and-holds moat is real and this roster is deeper than its record suggests | up

5. BaseballPro77 | Lost 5-3 but Murakami, Trout, and Basallo's .611 OBP all showed up again; the offense is consistently elite and Strahm's addition tightens the bullpen enough to turn close losses into wins | down

6. Athletic Park Dragons | 13 HR and 39 RBI and still lost 6-4 because the rotation posted a 6.31 ERA with zero quality starts — the offensive ceiling is among the league's best but the pitching is a five-alarm fire | down

7. North Correa | Glasnow posted a 0.25 WHIP and Sale a 0.50 WHIP and they still lost 5-4 — that's a brutal pill to swallow; Ohtani's elite multi-category output and 10 team steals keep this roster dangerous week to week | same

8. Riverside Roughnecks | Quietly one of the best stories of the week: beat a loaded Dragons squad with Kurtz (8 R, 3 HR), Suzuki (3 HR), and 5 QS from a rotation that is starting to look like a real strength | up

9. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Aranda's 3 HR and Griffin's dominant outing were encouraging, but a -0.43 luck delta and a reliance on a thin pitching staff means the 5-3 win may flatter this roster a touch | same

10. Canberra Wombats | Raleigh (4 HR) and Walker (3 HR) kept this team alive for a 5-5 tie but Nola's 11.00 ERA and a team ERA of 5.59 with just 2 QS underscore how fragile this rotation has become after dropping Sasaki | down

11. Buttville Skidmarks | De La Cruz and Caminero are a real power tandem and 5 NSVH is a genuine asset, but 24 runs scored and Bradish's 2.03 WHIP leave this roster feeling like it needs another consistent bat to push upward | same

12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got blown out 8-1 across nearly every pitching category, and dropping McGreevy without a clear replacement signals a front office without answers; this roster is trending the wrong direction | down

13. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | The -0.62 luck delta is now a recurring identity crisis — Harris, Moniak, and solid pitching all week produced only a tie; Dollander's addition offers rotation hope but the losses are piling up in the standings | same

14. Benchwarmers | A +1.33 luck delta is the most unsustainable number in the league; 3 HR, a 5.28 ERA, and dropping Scherzer and Assad leave this roster with very little margin for error going forward | same
week_06: 1. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | The 8-2 demolition of the Skeeters — with a new season-record .419 OBP, 12 HR, and 38 runs — is the kind of statement week that reshapes a season; the luck pendulum has finally swung and this lineup looks dangerous | up

2. Sugar Land Skeeters | An 8-2 blowout loss is jarring for the preseason favorite, but Chase Burns' 16 Ks, Matt Olson's 3 HR, and a 7 SB week remind you the talent floor here is still elite; one bad week doesn't erase five strong ones | down

3. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Kazuma Okamoto's 4 HR and 11 RBI, Ben Rice's 3 HR, and 12 team homers — the most explosive offensive week in the league this week — fueled a 6-4 win over Canberra; this team has been sleeping and just woke up | up

4. BaseballPro77 | Ranger Suarez was borderline untouchable at 0.50 WHIP with 13 Ks over 12 IP, Jordan Walker drove in 11, and the 7-3 win was convincing; a Week 7 showdown with General Hospital is the biggest game of the season so far | same

5. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Luzardo (1.35 ERA, 18 Ks), Clay Holmes (0.71 ERA), and 10 team quality starts are the most dominant pitching week any team has had all season; the 6-3 win was built on rotation excellence | up

6. Athletic Park Dragons | Aaron Judge's 3 HR and .556 OBP headlined a 7-2 win that showed what this lineup can do; Peter Lambert replaces Liberatore but the rotation still needs more before anyone trusts it in a playoff spot | same

7. Albuquerque Bombers | Byron Buxton's 5 HR week was a reminder of his ceiling and Zack Wheeler's 1.50 ERA kept things competitive, but the 7-3 loss to BaseballPro77 revealed real pitching depth concerns beyond Wheeler | down

8. General Hospital | The 6-3 loss stings despite 35 runs scored and a Vladdy-led offense generating real thunder; Max Fried was solid, but the team ERA of 3.76 and WHIP of 1.42 signal the rotation outside Fried isn't holding up its end — Jared Jones is a critical add | down

9. Riverside Roughnecks | Misiorowski's 0.38 WHIP, Langeliers' .542 OBP, and a disciplined 6-2 win over the Benchwarmers make this one of the quieter success stories of the week; the rotation is slowly becoming an asset | up

10. North Correa | Hancock and Sale each posted 1.29 ERAs in the same week and still lost 5-4 — that kind of rotation luck inversion is maddening; Bryson Stott and Luis Severino are smart adds but the offense needs to hit to support this staff | same

11. Hiroshima Radiation | A 5-4 squeaker over North Correa is a win, but 73 team strikeouts and only 3 HR and 14 RBI from the lineup is a troubling offensive imbalance; the pitching is real but this team cannot sustain without run support | same

12. Canberra Wombats | Pete Crow-Armstrong was excellent and Dylan Cease logged 12.2 innings, but 23 runs scored and a .316 OBP tell the story of an offense that simply isn't generating enough; Jake Bennett is a sensible rotation add but this team needs bats | down

13. Buttville Skidmarks | Bryan Reynolds' .469 OBP and Parker Messick's 15 Ks were bright spots in a 7-2 loss, but 22 runs scored and a 4.01 ERA say this roster is stuck in neutral; Spencer Steer is a useful add but not a difference-maker | same

14. Benchwarmers | The +1.58 luck delta is the loudest alarm bell in the league — Yesavage's 0.96 ERA was a genuine positive but one quality start, a 4.07 ERA, and an offense that scored 25 runs all week cannot sustain a winning record | same
week_07: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | A 9-1 demolition of Canberra with 12 HR, 36 RBI, 6 QS, and Eduardo Rodriguez at 0.59 ERA is the most complete week any team has had in weeks — the Skeeters are back and dangerous | up

2. Hiroshima Radiation | Bryce Harper's 4 HR and 7 runs, Schwarber's 5 HR, and a league-high 7 QS with a 2.58 ERA make this the most balanced roster in the league right now; the offense finally showed up alongside elite pitching | up

3. BaseballPro77 | Bobby Witt Jr.'s 3 HR and 7 runs, Bryce Elder's 1.54 ERA, and Logan Gilbert's 0.75 WHIP powered a 6-4 win over General Hospital; this team just won a measuring-stick game and looks like a genuine contender | same

4. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Davis Martin's 19 Ks and a team ERA of 1.71 — the new season record for lowest ERA by a winning team — are elite outputs; a 4-3 grind over Buttville with real pitching depth forming is a dangerous combination | up

5. Athletic Park Dragons | A 5-5 tie with the Bombers is a mild disappointment given Cristopher Sánchez's 0.67 WHIP and Aaron Judge's 3 HR, but the pitching staff remains the league's most improved and a QS total of 5 is a weekly anchor | down

6. Riverside Roughnecks | A convincing 7-3 win over the Twins backed by Seiya Suzuki, Xavier Edwards, and the massive waiver haul of Roki Sasaki, Bassitt, and Fedde signals real intent; this rotation could look completely different in two weeks | up

7. Albuquerque Bombers | A 5-5 tie when your pitching posts a 5.15 ERA and 1.47 WHIP is honestly a small miracle — Buxton's 3 HR, Naylor's 3 SB, and Turang's 2 HR kept it respectable, but the rotation is a serious concern heading into a Hiroshima matchup | down

8. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | The 3-7 loss hides a decent offensive week from Ramírez and Wilson, but dropping to zero NSVH and posting only 48 IP is unsustainable; the aggressive waiver wire work adding Dollander and Montero is the right call | down

9. General Hospital | Andy Pages' 4 HR and 8 RBI and Kyle Tucker's .478 OBP are trade-value peaks, but losing 6-4 despite 10 HR, 32 RBI, and 6 NSVH to BaseballPro77 stings — a Week 8 showdown with the Skeeters is now a must-win situation | down

10. North Correa | Posting a league-record-tying 12 SB, 31 runs, and 2 solid starts while still losing 5-4 is the definition of this team's season — elite speed and respectable pitching undercut by a total lack of power; 3 HR all week is unworkable | same

11. Benchwarmers | Cody Bellinger's 8 RBI and Dubón's 5 RBI are nice, but this team's +1.62 luck delta is an unsustainable house of cards; zero NSVH and a rotation with only 2 QS means the underlying talent cannot support their record long-term | same

12. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | Pete Alonso's 7 RBI, 6 QS, and 73 Ks are legitimate — this team is not as bad as their record suggests, and the -0.89 luck delta is a real injustice; Tyler Mahle adds rotation depth but they need the schedule to even out | up

13. Buttville Skidmarks | Eight NSVH and a 3.53 ERA are genuinely good pitching marks but losing 4-3 to a Reapers team with fewer innings and lower power output is a familiar story — this roster cannot score enough runs to win close matchups | same

14. Canberra Wombats | Getting blown out 9-1 with a .254 OBP and only 14 runs scored while dropping Jake Bennett off the roster is moving in the wrong direction on every front; deGrom's 4.05 ERA is concerning and the offense is the worst in the league this week | down
